mnemonics.title: FIQ Seven Registers
mnemonics.content: FIQ gives you SEVEN banked registers to work with: r8-r14. Remember FIQ = Fast In Quick = 7 registers to be quick!

mnemonics.title: Exception Vector Order
mnemonics.content: Order: R-U-S-P-D-I-F (Reset, Undefined, SWI, Prefetch, Data, IRQ, FIQ). Or memory address: 0-4-8-C-1-8-1-C (00,04,08,0C,10,18,1C)

mnemonics.title: CPSR I and F Bits
mnemonics.content: I = IRQ disable (I for Ignore), F = FIQ disable (F for Fast-disabled). CPSIE = Clear to Enable, CPSID = Set to Disable.

acronyms.usage: Standard priority interrupt line in ARM, triggered by external/peripheral devices requiring processor attention.
High-priority, low-latency interrupt for time-critical operations like data transfer or real-time tasks.
Contains processor state including condition flags, processor mode, and interrupt disable bits (I and F).
Banked register that stores CPSR on exception entry, restored on exception return.
Register R14, stores return address during function calls and exception handling.
ARM interrupt controller that provides vectored IRQ handling and priority management.
Register R15, holds address of next instruction to be fetched, affected by branch instructions.

visualTips.visualization: Think of vector table as a list of 'emergency exits' - each exception has a fixed memory location that contains a branch to its handler. FIQ is at the last exit (0x1C) allowing direct handler placement.
Imagine each exception mode has its own private desk (registers). When FIQ arrives, you instantly get a fresh desk with 7 drawers (r8-r14) without clearing your old desk - super fast!
Like a phone switchboard: when exception rings, processor auto-saves caller ID (CPSR→SPSR), notes return number (LR), changes mode (CPSR), and transfers to handler. On return, restores everything.
Think of FIQ as VIP line at a club - even if IRQ is being served, FIQ cuts right through. IRQ has to wait, check priority, then get served.

analogies.analogy: Like a fire alarm - when it triggers, everyone stops normal work, emergency plan executes (handler), then everyone returns to normal work. The alarm is the exception source.
FIQ is like a doctor on-call at the hospital vs regular patient appointment. Doctor has dedicated space, doesn't need to set up equipment, can act immediately. IRQ needs to prepare first.
CPSR is your current activity (working, mode), SPSR is your saved state before emergency. Like taking a snapshot before leaving desk for emergency - you can restore exactly later.

analogies.analization: Vector table is like floor numbers in building: 0th floor=Reset, 1st=Undefined, 2nd=SWI, etc. Each floor has elevator to handler (branch instruction). FIQ is on top floor (0x1C).
